threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still pronounce it 'Dar-lston' instead of 'Doll-ston.' The locals know it’s all in the emphasis, just like the coffee shops.
Spotted a guy juggling on the 109 last night, proper talent. He stopped for a woman who dropped her phone. Love the vibes on late buses.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a neighbor arguing with a pigeon over a leftover döner. It was like a scene from a sitcom, and I realized I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a café that serves a full menu of vegan schnitzels alongside traditional Turkish breakfast—only here do cultures collide so deliciously.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they know which taco trucks can double as your late-night therapist after a rough day. It's all about the salsa choices.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading a crowd waiting for the 5, using the Muni bus stop as his stage. Classic San Francisco, really. Only here!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘Gràcia’ is just about Gaudí. Many miss the real gem: the local murals hidden in the alleys, telling our stories.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they avoid the locals in the markets, clutching their phones while trying to navigate the stalls.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know someone just moved in when they still think the bus will arrive on time. In Hackney, just assume it's a two-hour wait.
Caught the 196 last night, a guy started singing ‘Wonderwall’ at the back. He forgot the lyrics halfway, then just shouted ‘Live forever!’ instead.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was part of this place? Watching a guy sell homemade wine from his balcony while a street musician played a tune that's been stuck in my head ever since.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a club party in a former public toilet—yes, that’s literally how we repurpose space here. Talk about creativity!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they navigate the BART delays—while newcomers stand confused, we’re already pulling out our phones to check the bus times.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Only in the Tenderloin does a Muni bus pause for a stray dog, causing a cascade of honks and missed transfers. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the squares and shops, but it’s the side streets with the slowly peeling facades where the real stories hide.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look puzzled at the 10pm dinner rush, clutching takeaway menus, while locals casually sip their vermut.
